rationale: Primary reading: a theory is a collection of closed sentences over a language, so def:sentence is load-bearing and was missing (def:language is the explicit parameter 'over L', kept). Secondary reading: the theory generated by a set of axioms Gamma is its deductive closure, so def:deductive-closure is load-bearing and was missing; def:axiom-system (Gamma as a chosen set of axioms) is kept.
licensing_quote:
is a collection of
closed sentences in that language
notes:
def:deductive-closure edge licensed by 'the theory generated by $\Gamma$ is the
deductive closure'. The underlying derivability relation '|-' has no node (see resolution-0021/0023). def:language kept as the 'over a first-order language L' parameter; if a precise node is wanted, def:first-order-language matches the wording. No cycle with def:deductive-closure: that node is defined from Gamma and |-, independent of 'theory'.
